titleStmt.title: SII 37.560: original edition by S. Nagarjuna and K. Munirathnam
No. 560. (A.R. No. B 64 of 1943-44.) Isakāla, hamlet of Nandikotkur Taluk, Kurnool District.

summary.p: Slab near the temple of Śiva on the way to Dudyāla.
This is dated Śaka 1486, Raktākshi, Māgha śu. 15, corresponding to 1565 A.D., January 16, Tuesday.

It records the grant of land at Isukāla as mānya by Rēmidēvuni Virūpayyaṁgāru of Nāgalūṭi to the artisans China-Vīrōju and Tarigoppula Pōchamōju for their having brought the images meant for processions for the temple of Kēśava at the village.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śālivāhana śakavarṣaṁ
bulu 1486 Agunēṭi
raktākṣi saṁvvatsara māgha śu
15 lu . śrīmadrājādhirāja
rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratā
pa sadāśiva mahārāyalu pṛ
thivi sāmrājyamu cēyucuṁ
ḍaṁgānu svasti samasta praśastisahi
tulaina śrīmatu śiddha bhikṣā vrittiyayya
vāri Āgñādārulaina rēmidēvuni nā
galūṭi viruŪpayyaṁgāru śrī
mallikārjuna mahādēvuni Amṛ
tapaḷaku cellivaccē yisukāla
lōnu kēśava perumāḷaku decci
yiccina vutsava vigrahālu te
cci vuṁḍagānu bhikṣāvritti nāga
lūṭi virupayyagāriki re
ḍi garaṇālu viṁnnapaṁ cēśi mā
nyaṁ yipiṁcinadi Agasā
la cinavīrōju tarigipu
la pōcamōjuku jana 2 kiṁnni
yiccina mānyaṁ cina ceru
vulōnu jaṁmi mānikini
sthaḷaṁ �3 nnu kālvu kaṭṭa stha
ḷaṁ �2 nnu vubhayaṁ sthaḷā
lu 2 Unu kṣētrāṁ kha 0 . 0 nnu
varimaḍi pātikegānu
kālu . kaṭlasthaḷaṁ 0 Ñ 0 nu
veraśi kṣētrā kha . 0 Ñ Akṣa
rālānu yeṁdumuṁnnu
yirusa Ākṣētramuṁnnu

mīku mānyaṁgānu
Istimi . Ācaṁdrārkka
mugānu Istimi tri
vācaka trikaraṇa śuddhi
gānu yistimi Ākṣē
traṁ palaparucuka
mī putra pautrapāraṁtra is written above the line.
paryayaṁttamugā
nu Anubhaviṁcci bra
tikēdi rmādi
sta śilāmaya
yattēna saṁrakṣa
ścaya . svada
ttā dvbiguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ paradattā
nu pālanaṁ paradattāpahārē
ṇa svadattaṁ niṣphalaṁ bhavē
t . svadattā vā paradattāvā
yō harēti vasuṁdharā
sahasrāṇi viṣṭā
krimi . Ida
Digital edition of SII 37.560 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
